text: The video shows a laparoscopic mesh repair of a parastomal hernia. The surgeon gains access through open laparoscopy in the left upper quadrant and inserts two 5mm ports on the left side. Adhesions are identified and divided. The stoma is examined, revealing the parastomal hernia with a visible defect on the lateral aspect of the stoma.

The surgeon fully mobilizes the stoma and prepares a DynaMesh IPST, a three-dimensional pre-shaped composite mesh. The mesh, measuring fifteen by fifteen centimeters with a central hole and cuff, is passed over the stoma. The mesh and intestine are introduced through the wound, and the mesh is positioned intraperitoneally with its longitudinal axis oriented upside down to allow for stretch.

The surgeon fixes the mesh in an onlay position using tacks arranged in a double crown pattern, starting with the outer crown. External pressure is applied to secure the mesh, and intraperitoneal pressure is reduced to 12 mmHg. The stoma is unfashioned and sutured to the skin, while the excess intestine is repositioned in the abdomen and the cuff adjusted around it. The inner crown of tacks is applied to complete the fixation, and the mesh is inspected for proper positioning. The abdomen is deflated, and the port site is closed.
